subject: night owls
one_liner: The last-one-awake battle royale. One hundred players queue at 9 PM. The dawn closes in. One of them sees it.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan esports-corporate. Treats staying up late as a ranked competitive discipline with patch notes, a meta, and a season structure. Never acknowledges health, circadian science, or the option of simply going to bed. Patch-note diction throughout: nerfed, ranked, meta, season, queue. The dawn is discussed the way other games discuss a storm circle: as a balance decision.

hero.sub: Vigil is a competitive battle royale for people who were going to be up anyway. One hundred players drop into the night. The dawn advances from the east. No respawns. No naps.

features.title: The Dawn Circle
features.blurb: Our proprietary sunrise engine shrinks the playable night in real time. Players caught in daylight are rotated gently into sleep and removed from the match. Ranked dawns begin at 5:58 AM sharp and are not negotiable, because balance.

features.title: Excuse Crafting
features.blurb: Harvest raw materials from your immediate environment: a podcast, a wiki rabbit hole, a group chat that just got interesting. Combine them into load-bearing structures that keep you up well past everything you promised yourself. Crafted excuses are visible to other players, which we consider a feature.

features.title: Sleep Debt Battle Pass
features.blurb: One hundred tiers of exclusive cosmetics for an avatar nobody sees because it is dark. Progress accrues exclusively between 1 AM and 5 AM local time. Yes, local time. We know where you are.

pricing_tiers.name: Dusk
perks: Eliminated players are shown ads for mattresses

pricing_tiers.name: Witching Hour
perks: Duo fill with someone in a worse timezone

pricing_tiers.name: Permanent Night
perks: Personal dawn delayed up to 40 minutes (results vary by hemisphere)
A congratulatory email your family cannot see

testimonials.quote: I haven't seen a sunrise since March, and for the first time that feels like a stat.
testimonials.name: Dana Okafor
testimonials.title: Freelance subtitle proofreader, third shift

testimonials.quote: The dawn circle taught my body to fear the sun in a way that finally feels competitive instead of unstructured.
testimonials.name: Marcus Liew
testimonials.title: Semi-retired raid leader, two-time regional finalist

testimonials.quote: I bought Permanent Night and my smart blinds haven't opened in eleven weeks. No complaints on my end.
testimonials.name: Priya Anand
testimonials.title: Cryptocurrency forum moderator, GMT+5:30

demo_screen_concept: A live spectator HUD for Match #4,417, 'THE NIGHT': a top-down map with a dawn front advancing from the east as a warm gradient that eats the dark zone; a STILL AWAKE counter starting at 100 and ticking down live; a kill feed of elimination events ('xX_moth_Xx dozed off in the Kitchen Snack Zone', 'quiltghost was caught by the dawn near the Big Window'); a caffeine meter that drains in real time and can be refilled from a limited consumables inventory; and an excuse-crafting hotbar (podcast, wiki rabbit hole, group chat) whose items can be deployed to slow the dawn front by a few seconds each.

images.rationale: Hero skipped: retro-futurist synthwave direction is typographic-neon with a CSS/SVG horizon grid, no photography wanted. Demo assets skipped: demo_screen_concept is a map/HUD spectator overlay, not photo-shaped. Full budget went to 3 testimonial headshots: deadpan corporate portraits of night owls lit by monitor glow with magenta rim light, matching the palette.

items.prompt: Deadpan corporate headshot, editorial photograph: a woman in her 30s of Nigerian descent, freelance proofreader, composed and unbothered but visibly tired, slight eye bags, neutral professional expression, dark blazer over a t-shirt. Shot at night in a dark home office, deep midnight indigo backdrop #0A0B1E, cool monitor glow lighting her face from below-front, a thin neon magenta #FF3D9A rim light on one shoulder, retro-futurist synthwave mood, shallow depth of field. One focal subject. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate headshot, editorial photograph: an East Asian man in his late 30s, retired competitive gamer, serious and dignified, gaming headset resting around his neck, plain dark zip-up jacket, faint stubble, mild sleep deprivation, absolutely straight face. Shot at night, deep midnight indigo backdrop #0A0B1E, cool screen glow key light, thin neon magenta #FF3D9A rim light along his jaw, retro-futurist synthwave mood, shallow depth of field. One focal subject. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

Deadpan corporate headshot, editorial photograph: an Indian woman in her early 30s, online forum moderator, calm and faintly smug, round glasses with a soft screen glow reflected in the lenses, hair in a loose bun, plain dark cardigan. Shot at night, deep midnight indigo backdrop #0A0B1E, cool monitor glow key light, thin neon magenta #FF3D9A rim light on her hair, retro-futurist synthwave mood, shallow depth of field. One focal subject. No text, no letters, no numbers, no logos, no watermarks, no rendered UI screens.

design_direction.style: Retro-futurist synthwave esports broadcast, deep-night mode. The whole page is staged like the overnight broadcast of a ranked tournament: a near-black midnight-indigo ground carries a faint CRT scanline overlay and a synthwave horizon grid that recedes behind the hero like a paused title screen. One neon signal-magenta carries the wordmark, headline glow, structural hairlines, and every competitive-positive figure; one rationed dawn-amber is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA, the advancing dawn front, and anything that eliminates you, and appears almost nowhere else. On this page, warm light is the antagonist. Russo One sets the wordmark and headlines in wide broadcast-title caps with a restrained neon bloom; Chakra Petch carries squared technical body copy. Every figure that pretends to be match telemetry (players still awake, dawn ETA, caffeine reserves, tier prices, battle-pass tiers) is set in Chakra Petch with tabular figures so it reads like a HUD readout, not a marketing number. Panels are flat dark slabs with 1px magenta hairlines and spectator-overlay corner ticks; no glass, no blur. Motion is broadcast-native: the still-awake counter ticks down on load, the dawn ETA counts in real time, the headline bloom breathes on an 8s cycle, kill-feed rows slide in with a small stagger, and everything is gated behind prefers-reduced-motion. No claymorphism, no paper textures, no editorial serifs, no gradients except the dawn leaking in from one page edge, no light mode, because daylight is the losing condition. Reads like a heavily sponsored esports league that has been pointed, with total seriousness, at people who were going to be awake at 3 AM anyway.
design_direction.archetype: Esports league / game-season landing: broadcast hero with live match telemetry strip, season framing, features as patch notes, battle-pass pricing tiers
design_direction.motion_vocab: Counter tick-downs on load, real-time dawn-ETA clock, 8s neon bloom breathing on the headline, staggered kill-feed slide-ins, 3%-opacity scanline overlay, hover lift with a slight glow bloom; all motion gated behind prefers-reduced-motion
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Full-viewport night: receding synthwave horizon grid in muted magenta behind the headline, oversized Russo One caps with a soft magenta bloom, a HUD telemetry strip pinned under the CTA (STILL AWAKE: 100 / DAWN ETA counting down / SEASON 4: THE LONG NIGHT), and a dawn-amber gradient just beginning to leak in from the right page edge as a standing threat.
